rationale: The only direct linkage is a single DOI-matched reference entry, with no in-text citation context or reference text provided. There is plausible methodological overlap (both papers use weekly scanner data with dynamic time-series elements such as unit-root testing and impulse-response-style analysis), but the cited paper’s focus (share–distribution feedback in a new category) is not obviously central to the citing paper’s main promotion-effect question. Given this sparse and mixed evidence, it is not safe to drop, but importance is not clear.
